title: Getting started with eemr
text: eemr R/Rcpp binding original eemCpp command-line tool. implements coherence-based expression module (EEM) search: given expression matrix collection candidate gene sets (e.g. MSigDB), finds, gene set, largest subset genes whose expression coherent across samples (“expression module”), assigns module significance p-value based randomization test. ’s conceptually similar gene set enrichment analysis, “signal” tested coherence expression rather differential expression, coherent subset gene set retained. whole pipeline runs memory R – intermediate files, external CLI binary.

title: Getting started with eemr
text: eem_search() takes two inputs, already loaded R: expr, numeric matrix, geneSets, named list character vectors. commonly distributed plain-text files, package bundles small real example provides read_gmt() read gene set file.

title: Getting started with eemr
text: tab-delimited text file: first line header giving sample names (empty first field), following line one gene – name, one tab-separated numeric value per sample: exactly format read read.table(file, header = TRUE, row.names = 1, sep = "\t", check.names = FALSE), bundled test.tab loaded . check.names = FALSE keeps sample names aren’t valid R identifiers (e.g. starting digit) unchanged. result coerced plain numeric matrix .matrix() eem_search() requires matrix, data.frame.

title: Getting started with eemr
text: tab-delimited GMT file, format used MSigDB/GSEA: one gene set per line, gene set id, description (unused , required format – "na" don’t one), one gene id per remaining field: read_gmt(file) reads named list, e.g. list(GENE_SET_1 = c("GENE1", "GENE2", "GENE3"), ...), suitable eem_search()’s geneSets argument. package bundles small real dataset (1000 genes x 118 breast cancer samples, 100 candidate GMT gene sets) examples testing:
code: GENE_SET_1  na  GENE1   GENE2   GENE3 GENE_SET_2  na  GENE4   GENE5   GENE6   GENE7 expr_file <- system.file("extdata", "test.tab", package = "eemr") gmt_file  <- system.file("extdata", "test.gmt", package = "eemr")  expr <- as.matrix(read.table(expr_file, header = TRUE, row.names = 1,                               sep = "\t", check.names = FALSE)) geneSets <- read_gmt(gmt_file)  dim(expr) #> [1] 1000  118 length(geneSets) #> [1] 100

title: Getting started with eemr
text: result data.frame one row per gene set passed search, sorted decreasing pvalue (significant first): pvalue1 approximate, hypergeometric-based p-value used screen candidate gene sets cheaply. pvalue2 accurate, permutation-based p-value, calculated gene sets pass pvalue1Cutoff screen (-1 means wasn’t calculated). pvalue one actually reported (pvalue2 available, otherwise pvalue1). three -log10 scale, larger values significant. seedGenes/moduleGenes list gene set’s original genes coherent subset found search (;-separated). Key parameters (see ?eem_search): relativeRadius: target fraction genes fall within module search radius (default 0.05). pvalue1Cutoff: -log10 cutoff used screen candidates accurate p-value calculated (default -log10(0.05)); use -1 disable screening. itr: number permutations accurate p-value (default 300).
code: res <- eem_search(expr, geneSets) head(res[, c("id", "pvalue", "pvalue1", "pvalue2",              "nSeedGenes", "nModuleGenes")], 10) #>                                    id    pvalue   pvalue1   pvalue2 nSeedGenes #> 1                             SPINDLE 14.809837 13.408965 14.809837         10 #> 2            MICROTUBULE_CYTOSKELETON 11.518622 10.411940 11.518622         18 #> 3                   CYTOSKELETAL_PART  9.007283  8.155555  9.007283         26 #> 4                        CYTOSKELETON  7.872272  6.716510  7.872272         34 #> 5        NON_MEMBRANE_BOUND_ORGANELLE  7.646947  6.903397  7.646947         40 #> 6                      ORGANELLE_PART  6.781448  5.921035  6.781448         57 #> 7           EXTRACELLULAR_MATRIX_PART  6.030158  4.818086  6.030158         13 #> 8                            MEMBRANE  5.781970  5.120973  5.781970        149 #> 9  PROTEINACEOUS_EXTRACELLULAR_MATRIX  5.131244  4.253077  5.131244         30 #> 10                            NUCLEUS  3.883952  2.955901  3.883952         67 #>    nModuleGenes #> 1            10 #> 2            11 #> 3            11 #> 4            11 #> 5            12 #> 6            13 #> 7             6 #> 8            20 #> 9             8 #> 10           10

title: Getting started with eemr
text: modules, module_activity() turns module x sample matrix, useful downstream analyses (clustering samples, correlating clinical variables, etc.). module averages (row-standardized, default) expression moduleGenes across samples: default modules pvalue >= 6 (-log10 scale, .e. p < 1e-6) included; pass pvalueCutoff = NULL include every module res, -log10 threshold: quick look top modules’ activity varies across samples:
code: activity <- module_activity(expr, res) dim(activity) #> [1]   7 118 activity[1:5, 1:5] #>                                 b0359     s0018      s0107    s0183     b0318 #> SPINDLE                      1.455746 0.4958813 -0.4685343 1.810715 0.3980437 #> MICROTUBULE_CYTOSKELETON     1.341880 0.6116425 -0.5466202 1.753574 0.4379854 #> CYTOSKELETAL_PART            1.341880 0.6116425 -0.5466202 1.753574 0.4379854 #> CYTOSKELETON                 1.341880 0.6116425 -0.5466202 1.753574 0.4379854 #> NON_MEMBRANE_BOUND_ORGANELLE 1.403883 0.6201511 -0.5539144 1.821083 0.4509757 nrow(module_activity(expr, res, pvalueCutoff = NULL)) #> [1] 23 nrow(module_activity(expr, res, pvalueCutoff = 10)) #> [1] 2 heatmap(activity, scale = "none", margins = c(2, 12),         labCol = FALSE, main = "Module activity")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Atsushi Niida. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Niida (2026). eemr: Coherence-Based Expression Module (EEM) Search. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/atusiniida/eemr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {eemr: Coherence-Based Expression Module (EEM) Search},   author = {Atsushi Niida},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/atusiniida/eemr}, }

title: Coherence-Based Expression Module (EEM) Search
text: R bindings (via Rcpp) original eemCpp coherence-based expression module (EEM) search tool. Given expression matrix collection candidate gene sets, eem_search() finds coherently expressed subset gene set (“expression module”) assigns significance p-value, without leaving R (temp files, CLI binary).

title: Coherence-Based Expression Module (EEM) Search
text: repository public, GitHub token needed: local clone instead:
code: # install.packages("remotes")  # if not already installed remotes::install_github("atusiniida/eemr") devtools::install("path/to/eemr")

title: Coherence-Based Expression Module (EEM) Search
text: See inst/examples/run_test.R full example using bundled sample dataset (inst/extdata/test.tab, test.gmt), “Getting started” vignette walkthrough.
code: library(eemr)  expr <- as.matrix(read.table("expr.tab", header = TRUE, row.names = 1,                               sep = "\t", check.names = FALSE)) geneSets <- read_gmt("genesets.gmt")  res <- eem_search(expr, geneSets) head(res[, c("id", "pvalue", "nSeedGenes", "nModuleGenes")])  # module x sample activity matrix (mean row-standardized expression of each # module's genes), restricted by default to modules with pvalue >= 6 # (-log10 scale, i.e. p < 1e-6) activity <- module_activity(expr, res)

title: Coherence-Based Expression Module (EEM) Search
text: eem_search(expr, geneSets, ...) — run EEM search read_gmt(file) — read GMT gene set file named list module_activity(expr, result, normalize = TRUE, pvalueCutoff = 6) — build module x sample activity matrix eem_search() results

title: Coherence-based Expression Module (EEM) search — eem_search
text: gene set geneSets, finds largest coherently expressed subset ("expression module") among genes assigns p-value: approximate, hypergeometric-based p-value (pvalue1) first used screen candidates, accurate permutation-based p-value (pvalue2) calculated pass screen. pvalue one actually used (pvalue2 available, otherwise pvalue1); p-values -log10 scale, larger values significant.

title: Coherence-based Expression Module (EEM) search — eem_search
code: eem_search(   expr,   geneSets,   relativeRadius = 0.05,   pvalue1Cutoff = -log10(0.05),   itr = 300,   minGeneSetSize = 10,   maxGeneSetSize = 2000,   coreGeneSize = 10 )

title: Coherence-based Expression Module (EEM) search — eem_search
text: expr numeric matrix expression values genes rows samples columns. Must rownames (gene ids); row internally standardized (mean 0, sd 1) search. geneSets named list character vectors, e.g. list(SET1 = c("gene1", "gene2"), SET2 = c("gene3", "gene4")). Names used gene set ids. See read_gmt load GMT file. relativeRadius target fraction genes expr fall within module search radius (default 0.05). pvalue1Cutoff cutoff (-log10 scale) approximate p-value used screen candidate gene sets accurate, permutation-based p-value calculated. Default -log10(0.05). Use -1 (negative value) disable screening calculate accurate p-values every gene set. itr number permutations used calculate accurate p-value (default 300). minGeneSetSize, maxGeneSetSize gene sets whose size, intersecting genes present expr, falls outside [minGeneSetSize, maxGeneSetSize] dropped (defaults 10 2000). coreGeneSize number genes closest module center used refine center (default 10).

title: Coherence-based Expression Module (EEM) search — eem_search
text: data.frame one row per gene set passed search,   columns id, pvalue, pvalue1, pvalue2,   nSeedGenes, nModuleGenes, absoluteRadius,   relativeRadius, seedGenes/moduleGenes (gene ids   collapsed ";"), sorted decreasing pvalue.

title: Coherence-based Expression Module (EEM) search — eem_search
code: expr_file <- system.file("extdata", "test.tab", package = "eemr") gmt_file  <- system.file("extdata", "test.gmt", package = "eemr") expr <- as.matrix(read.table(expr_file, header = TRUE, row.names = 1,                               sep = "\t", check.names = FALSE)) geneSets <- read_gmt(gmt_file) res <- eem_search(expr, geneSets) head(res[, c("id", "pvalue", "nSeedGenes", "nModuleGenes")]) #>                             id    pvalue nSeedGenes nModuleGenes #> 1                      SPINDLE 14.809837         10           10 #> 2     MICROTUBULE_CYTOSKELETON 11.518622         18           11 #> 3            CYTOSKELETAL_PART  9.007283         26           11 #> 4                 CYTOSKELETON  7.872272         34           11 #> 5 NON_MEMBRANE_BOUND_ORGANELLE  7.646947         40           12 #> 6               ORGANELLE_PART  6.781448         57           13

what: eemr: Coherence-Based Expression Module Search — eemr-package
title: eemr: Coherence-Based Expression Module Search — eemr-package
text: R bindings (via Rcpp) eemCpp coherence-based expression module search tool. Given expression matrix collection candidate gene sets, eem_search() finds coherently expressed subsets gene set ("expression modules") assigns significance p-value based permutation test.

title: eemr: Coherence-Based Expression Module Search — eemr-package
text: Maintainer: Atsushi Niida atusiniida@gmail.com Authors: Atsushi Niida atusiniida@gmail.com

what: Compute a module x sample activity matrix — module_activity
title: Compute a module x sample activity matrix — module_activity
text: expression module returned eem_search, computes "activity" sample mean expression module genes (moduleGenes column), giving matrix one row per module one column per sample.

title: Compute a module x sample activity matrix — module_activity
Compute a module x sample activity matrix — module_activity
text: expr numeric matrix (genes x samples, gene rownames) passed eem_search. result data.frame returned eem_search (must id moduleGenes columns). normalize standardize gene (row expr) mean 0, sd 1 averaging (default TRUE), matching row-normalization eem_search() applies internally searching modules, genes different expression scales contribute comparably module's activity score. Set FALSE average raw expression values instead. pvalueCutoff include modules whose pvalue (-log10 scale, convention pvalue1Cutoff eem_search) least value. Default 6 (.e. p < 1e-6). Set NULL include every module result.

title: Compute a module x sample activity matrix — module_activity
text: numeric matrix one row per included module (named   result$id) ncol(expr) columns (named   colnames(expr)).

title: Compute a module x sample activity matrix — module_activity
code: expr_file <- system.file("extdata", "test.tab", package = "eemr") gmt_file  <- system.file("extdata", "test.gmt", package = "eemr") expr <- as.matrix(read.table(expr_file, header = TRUE, row.names = 1,                               sep = "\t", check.names = FALSE)) geneSets <- read_gmt(gmt_file) res <- eem_search(expr, geneSets) activity <- module_activity(expr, res) dim(activity) #> [1]   7 118 # only keep modules significant at p < 0.01 (-log10(0.01) = 2) activity <- module_activity(expr, res, pvalueCutoff = -log10(0.01))

what: Read a GMT gene set file — read_gmt
title: Read a GMT gene set file — read_gmt
text: Reads tab-delimited GMT file (used MSigDB/GSEA: one gene set per line, columns id, description, gene1, gene2, ...) named list suitable eem_search.

title: Read a GMT gene set file — read_gmt
Read a GMT gene set file — read_gmt
text: file path GMT file.

title: Read a GMT gene set file — read_gmt
text: named list character vectors (gene set id -> gene ids).
